data.title: Create proposal schema prompt defining structured proposal format
data.description:
## Description

Create plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/prompts/proposal-schema.md defining the structured proposal format shared between implementation-plan skill and the decision-maker agent.

**Content**:
1. Structured proposal format: title, description, files likely touched, pros, cons, risk profile
2. Risk profile categories (low/medium/high with criteria for each)
3. Distinctness criteria: proposals must differ on at least one structural axis — data layer, control flow, dependency graph, or interface boundary
4. Distinctness validation gate: how to verify proposals are structurally different (compare on 4 axes, reject if all axes are identical)

This is the single source of truth for proposal format — shared between the skill (Story 68f6-9130) and the decision-maker agent (Story a1f3-db49).

**Unit test exemption**: No conditional logic (pure schema specification consumed by LLM, not executable code). Any test would be a change-detector test asserting text exists. Infrastructure-boundary only (reference document). Schema correctness is implicitly verified at GREEN by Task 4, when contract tests and eval pass against the completed SKILL.md.

## Files to modify
- plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/prompts/proposal-schema.md (create)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] File exists
  Verify: test -f plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/prompts/proposal-schema.md
- [ ] Contains all 6 format fields (title, description, files, pros, cons, risk)
  Verify: for f in title description files pros cons risk; do grep -qi "$f" plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/prompts/proposal-schema.md || exit 1; done
- [ ] Contains distinctness criteria with structural axes
  Verify: grep -qi "distinct" plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/prompts/proposal-schema.md
